frames: The agent, a T-shaped object, stands upright with its horizontal bar resting on the ground, then begins to tilt slightly to the right.
The agent continues to tilt smoothly to the right, with its vertical bar angling further away from the upright position.
The agent's vertical bar is now significantly angled, and the horizontal bar maintains contact with the ground, acting as a pivot.
The agent's vertical bar is nearly parallel to the ground, and the horizontal bar is still the primary point of contact, sliding slightly.
The agent is almost entirely horizontal, with the vertical bar approaching the ground, and the horizontal bar still supporting the fall.
The agent's vertical bar makes contact with the ground, and the entire structure is now lying flat on the surface.
The agent is fully resting on the ground, with both its horizontal and vertical bars lying flat and stationary.
The agent remains motionless, lying completely flat on the checkered ground, having completed its fall.
